data.title: Aduro Clean Technologies GAAP EPS of C$-0.21 misses by C$0.09, revenue of C$0.12M
data.description:
• Aduro Clean Technologies press release (ADUR): Q2 GAAP EPS of C$-0.21 misses by C$0.09.
  • Revenue of C$0.12M (+200.0% Y/Y).

data.title: Bit Origin to effect 1-for-60 reverse stock split
data.description:
Bit Origin (BTOG) on Thursday announced a 1-for-60 reverse stock split, effective January 20, 2026.

"The reverse stock split is intended to support our efforts to maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550((a))(2)," said CEO Jinghai Jiang.

New CUSIP number of the class A shares will be G21621134.

The Singapore-based cryptocurrency miner said the share combination will reduce its outstanding class A shares to ~1.5M from ~88.6M. Meanwhile, class B shares will reduce to 12,800 shares from 768,000.

data.title: OpenAI invests in brain computer interface startup Merge Labs
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) announced on Thursday that it has invested in brain computer interface startup Merge Labs, which includes OpenAI CEO Sam Altman as an investor.

Brain computer interfaces, or BCIs, allow for new ways to communicate, learn, and interact with technology, OpenAI said in a statement. “BCIs will create a natural, human-centered way for anyone to seamlessly interact with AI,” the ChatGPT maker added. “This is why OpenAI is participating in Merge Labs’ seed round.”

OpenAI continued: “AI will play a central role to Merge’s approach. It will accelerate research and development including bioengineering, neuroscience and device engineering. In addition, high-bandwidth interfaces will benefit from AI operating systems that can interpret intent, adapt to individuals, and operate reliably with limited and noisy signals. OpenAI will collaborate with Merge Labs on scientific foundation models and other frontier tools to accelerate progress.”

OpenAI did not disclose how much money it put into Merge Labs, which raised $252M in the funding round, however, TechCrunch reported it wrote the largest check among the investors. 

Merge Lab's website is sparse, but it describes itself as a “research lab striving to bring together the smartest, most motivated people building the future of BCI.”

“Together, we will push the frontiers of molecular engineering, hardware and understanding of brain function at scale,” MergeLabs added. “We will update our technical approaches based on data and seek to shorten timeframes. Our ultimate measure of success is creating real products that people love – initially to help patients with injury or disease and later to more broadly advance human capability.”

Other notable brain computer interface startups include Neuralink, which is run by Elon Musk, who co-founded OpenAI in 2015 along with Altman. Musk announced earlier this month that Neuralink will start high-volume production of brain-computer interface devices this year.

Neuralink is aiming for $1B in annual revenue by 2030, according to previous media reports.

data.title: xAI, Grok backlash continues as Canada expands probe into sexualized images
data.description:
The Privacy Commissioner of Canada announced on Thursday he is expanding an investigation into X regarding its chatbot Grok, created by xAI (X.AI), being used to generate sexualized images of women and children without their consent.

Privacy Commissioner Philippe Dufresne initially launched an investigation into X, which is run by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, last February, when his office received its first complaint on the matter.

The investigation will try to determine if X and xAI has violated Canada's federal privacy law, the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act.

"More specifically, the expanded investigations will consider whether X Corp. and xAI have obtained valid consent from individuals for the collection, use, and disclosure of their personal information to create deepfakes, including explicit content, via Grok and whether the companies have collected, used, and disclosed this information in accordance with the Act," according to the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada.

"The use of personal information without consent to create deepfakes, including intimate images, is a growing phenomenon that poses serious risks to individuals' fundamental right to privacy," Dufresne said. "I have decided to expand my investigation to address this issue given its importance and the potential serious harms that it may cause to Canadians."

This expanded probe follows a host of similar investigations launched recently around the globe.

On Wednesday, California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced an investigation to determine if xAI has violated the law by allowing Grok to generate these images. Earlier this month, a group of U.S. senators asked Apple (AAPL) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) to remove Grok and X apps from their digital stores.

The U.K. has also initiated an investigation into X due to "deeply concerning reports of the Grok AI chatbot account on X being used to create and share undressed images of people."

Malaysia's communications regulator said it will take legal action against X and xAI over the issue as well.

However, Musk and xAI said they have already addressed the problem.

"We now geoblock the ability of all users to generate images of real people in bikinis, underwear, and similar attire via the Grok account and in Grok in X in those jurisdictions where it's illegal," xAI posted on Wednesday. 

"When asked to generate images, it will refuse to produce anything illegal, as the operating principle for Grok is to obey the laws of any given country or state," Musk said. "There may be times when adversarial hacking of Grok prompts does something unexpected. If that happens, we fix the bug immediately."

data.title: Europe’s defense boom opens IPO window for arms makers
data.description:
Europe’s defense sector has been one of the region’s strongest equity themes for more than a year, and the rally is now pushing private arms manufacturers toward public markets.

Among the most prominent is Czechoslovak Group, a major supplier of ammunition and armored vehicles to Ukraine, which said this week it plans to pursue a stock market listing in Amsterdam. The offering could become the largest initial public offering ever by a defense company. Separately, KNDS, the maker of Germany’s Leopard 2 tank, is also weighing a potential IPO later this year.

The move toward public listings comes as European governments sharply increase military spending following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and pressure from Washington for NATO members to strengthen their defenses. Much of that spending is flowing to domestic manufacturers. A basket of European defense stocks tracked by Goldman Sachs has more than doubled over the past year, reflecting expectations that demand for weapons, ammunition and military vehicles will exceed Europe’s production capacity.

Analysts say the current market backdrop is unusually favorable for defense listings, with strong investor appetite and limited sector exposure available through public markets, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

Beyond raising capital or allowing shareholders to exit, companies see additional benefits in going public. Listed firms can use equity as acquisition currency, while the disclosure and credibility associated with a public listing may help win customers and sensitive government contracts.

Recent defense IPOs have already shown the potential upside. Shares of Renk (RNKGF), which produces gearboxes for military vehicles, along with night-vision specialists Theon International (TNIPF) and Exosens (EXOSF), have all climbed well above their debut prices since listing last year.

The pipeline extends beyond traditional arms makers. Finnish satellite company Iceye is considering an IPO as demand grows for space-based intelligence. Poland’s WB Electronics and Germany-based Vincorion are also exploring listings, while Norway’s Kongsberg Gruppen (NSKFF) is preparing a spinoff of its maritime unit to sharpen its focus on defense.

Some investors question how durable the rally will be, particularly if the war in Ukraine moves toward a negotiated settlement, which could pressure valuations. Others argue the opportunity is structural rather than cyclical, driven by a long-term gap between Europe’s military capabilities and NATO requirements.

Portfolio managers note that even without an active conflict, the scale of Europe’s rearmament needs suggests defense spending and industry growth could extend for many years, underpinning continued investor interest in the sector.

data.title: Trump unveils 'Great Healthcare Plan' to lower drug prices, premiums
data.description:
President Trump has finally unveiled his long-awaited plan to reform healthcare, dubbed the "Great Healthcare Plan," that focuses on codifying the "most favored nation" deals for cheaper drugs from pharmas as well as funding a cost-sharing reduction program for health insurance plans.

The GHP also calls for ending kickbacks paid by pharmacy benefit managers to brokerage middlemen.

In addition to codifying the MFN program, the plan also calls for having more prescription drugs available for over-the-counter purchase. "This will lower healthcare costs and increase consumer choice by strengthening price transparency, increasing competition, and reducing the need for costly and time-consuming doctor's visits," according to a White House fact sheet.

Regarding insurance premiums, for those who get their coverage through state health exchanges, subsidy payments would be given to individuals directly instead of insurers to buy a plan of their choosing.         

A cost-sharing reduction program would save taxpayers at least $36B—though over what period is unclear—and cut the most common ACA plan premiums by more than 10%, the White House said, citing the Congressional Budget Office. Details were not provided.     

Insurers will also have to provide information in "Plain English" rather than "industry jargon" to make them more accountable. They will also need to prominently publish rate and coverage comparisons, the percentage of their revenues that are paid out to claims compared to overhead costs and profits, and the frequency of claim denials.

data.title: Paramount fails to get its suit against Warner Bros. expedited: report
data.description:
A Delaware judge refused to expedite Paramount Skydance Corp.’s (PSKY) lawsuit that accused directors of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. (WBD) of misleading investors about a more than $82.7B buyout bid from Netflix Inc. (NFLX), Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

According to the report, Judge Morgan Zurn found that Paramount’s demands didn’t meet legal tests for fast-tracking a hearing into accusations that Warner Bros.' board made erroneous disclosures about the deal.

Paramount failed to show it would "suffer irreparable harm" by Warner Bros.' allegedly deficient disclosures, Zurn said. The judge explained that as a stockholder, Paramount can’t be harmed directly by a lack of disclosures since it won’t be making any decision on its own tender offer.

Paramount filed the suit earlier this week to obtain more details about Warner Bros.' deal with Netflix. The David Ellison-led company wants Warner Bros. to correct misleading information about the deal before investors vote on it.The case is Paramount v. Zaslav, 2026-0044, Delaware Chancery Court (Wilmington).

data.title: Alamos slides as 2025 revenue hit record but gold production missed guidance
data.description:
Alamos Gold (AGI) -6.7% in Thursday's trading after reporting FY 2025 gold production fell 3.8% Y/Y to 545,400 oz, missing revised guidance of 560K-580K oz, and Q4 output of 141,500 oz, roughly flat from 141,700 oz in Q3 and slightly above 140,200 oz in the year-ago quarter.

Alamos (AGI) said lower than expected production from the Island Gold district and Young-Davidson mine weighed on full-year results, and severe weather in December disrupted access to sites and affected mining and processing rates.

Island Gold produced 250,400 oz for the year and 60K oz in Q4, Young-Davidson produced 153,400 oz for the year and 41,500 oz in the quarter, and the Mulatos district contributed 141,600 oz for the year and 40K oz in Q4.

At the same time, Alamos (AGI) set revenue records for FY 2025 and Q4, with full-year sales totaling 531,230 oz of gold at an average realized price of $3,372/oz to generate revenues of $1.8B, and Q4 sales of 142,149 oz of gold at an average realized price of $3,997/oz for revenues of $568M.

"From an operational perspective, this past year was not reflective of our long-term track record, nor our strong outlook," President and CEO John McCluskey said. "We expect a substantial improvement from our operations in 2026 and low-cost growth from the Island Gold district to drive production significantly higher over the next several years."

data.title: Diageo is viewed positively by RBC Capital as the focus shifts to the mainstream part of the business
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets sees upside for Diageo plc (DEO) with the new CEO expected to revitalize the mainstream business. 

Analyst James Edwardes Jones highlighted that Diageo (DEO) enjoys an enviable stable of brands across multiple price points, although it was noted the company has overemphasized its luxury segment, with the brands now becoming increasingly unaffordable. "We think that reviving the neglected mainstream will be the key to Diageo rediscovering sustainable volume growth," updated Edwardes Jones.

Notably, the mainstream part of the alcohol business is where incoming CEO Sir Dave Lewis has spent his career. His lack of experience in the spirits sector is not expected by Edwardes Jones to be an issue as he implements his strategy changes.

Still, Diageo (DEO) is anticipated to see some pressure on price mix and margins, even as plentiful cost-saving opportunities arise. Positive catalysts seen for 2026 include the first-half fiscal year results to be reported in February and a potential Capital Markets Day.

RBC Capital assigned a price target of £20 to DEO to represent more than 16% upside potential.

Shares of Diageo (DEO) turned 1.4% lower in late morning trading.

data.title: State Street said to launch tokenized products in crypto push
data.description:
State Street (STT) is moving deeper into digital assets as the custody bank is developing a suite of tokenized products, according to a Thursday media report.

The company is specifically rolling out money-market and exchange-traded funds and cash products like tokenized deposits and stablecoins, Bloomberg reported, citing an emailed statement.

State Street (STT) will work with its money managers and institutional clients, in addition to its asset management division, Bloomberg reported. That unit last month teamed up with Mike Novogratz's Galaxy Digital (GLXY) to launch a tokenized private liquidity fund. State Street already takes care of administration and accounting for clients' digital-asset holdings and also crypto ETFs.

The move comes as big financial firms are expanding in crypto as the Trump administration take a more supportive stance. BNY (BK), another custody lender, rolled out a money market fund tailored to stablecoin issuers.

State Street (STT) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.
